subtitleNullable: UAV Cyber Intrusion Detection Dataset (DoS & Replay Attacks)
descriptionNullable:
This dataset is a derived and simplified version of the Cyber-Physical Dataset for UAVs Under Normal Operations and Cyberattacks, originally published by Hassler et al. (2023).

Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in mission-critical applications, making them vulnerable to cyber intrusions targeting wireless communication links. Machine learning-based intrusion detection systems (IDSs) have shown promising results in mitigating such threats. However, the complexity and heterogeneity of cyber-physical datasets can hinder reproducibility and focused analysis.

To address this, this dataset provides a cleaned and cyber-only subset of the original dataset, enabling efficient development and evaluation of network-based IDS models for UAV systems.

✂️ **Modifications from the Original Dataset**

The following preprocessing steps were applied:

✅ Removed all physical (telemetry) features, focusing exclusively on network-level cyber features.

✅ Excluded False Data Injection (FDI) and Evil Twin attacks, as these attacks primarily manifest at the cyber-physical or infrastructure level.

✅ Retained only Benign, DoS, and Replay traffic, representing:

Normal UAV communication

Volume-based attacks

Stealthy packet manipulation attacks

✅ Removed empty and non-informative columns to improve dataset consistency.

These modifications allow researchers to focus on pure cyber intrusion detection without the need for sensor fusion or physical-layer analysis.

🧠 **Attack Types Included**
| Class             | Description                                                              |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Benign**        | Normal UAV communication without attacks                                 |
| **DoS Attack**    | De-authentication denial-of-service attacks targeting UAV communication  |
| **Replay Attack** | Replayed legitimate packets to deceive the UAV or ground control station |

This selection covers a representative spectrum of cyber threats commonly studied in IDS research.

📊 **Dataset Characteristics**
Data Type: Network traffic (Cyber only)

Format: CSV (single file)

Features: Wi-Fi (WLAN), frame-level, and timing features

Label Column: class

Suitable for:

Binary classification (Normal vs Attack)

Multi-class classification (Benign, DoS, Replay)

Machine Learning and Deep Learning-based IDS models

🔬 **Recommended Use Cases**

UAV network intrusion detection

Benchmarking ML vs DL models

Feature selection and traffic analysis

Lightweight IDS design for UAV systems

📎 **Original Dataset & Paper**

This dataset is derived from the following work:

Hassler, S. C., Mughal, U. A., & Ismail, M. (2023).
Cyber-Physical Intrusion Detection System for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles.
IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems.

🔗 Original repository:
https://github.com/uamughal/UAVs-Dataset-Under-Normal-and-Cyberattacks

🔗 IEEE DataPort version:
https://ieee-dataport.org

📌 **Citation**

If you use this dataset, please cite the original work:

@article{hassler2023cyber,
  title={Cyber-Physical Intrusion Detection System for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles},
  author={Hassler, Samuel Chase and Mughal, Umair Ahmad and Ismail, Muhammad},
  journal={IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems},
  year={2023},
  publisher={IEEE}
}
